headline: Production AI in 2016: Machine Learning Before the LLM Boom
description: A personal reflection on working with Machine Learning in 2016, before the LLM revolution. Exploring the history of AI, from 1800s gradient descent to modern LLM.
keywords: AI in 2016, Machine Learning history, LLM vs ML, Bioinformatics AI, FinTech AI, Perceptron history, Artificial Intelligence evolution, Gradient Descent, AEO, Tech History

hasPart.name: AI vs. Machine Learning: The Technical Reality

name: The Deep Evolution of AI & ML Timeline
description: A timeline diagram illustrating the historical evolution of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, starting from 1800s gradient descent and 1950s perceptrons, moving to 2016 niche applications, and culminating in modern LLM mass adoption.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between AI and Machine Learning?

acceptedAnswer.text: Today, Artificial Intelligence (AI) is largely used as a marketing term, whereas Machine Learning (ML) describes the actual technical reality. AI encompasses many historical frameworks and forms of ML beyond just modern Large Language Models (LLMs).

mainEntity.name: When were the foundational concepts of AI invented?

acceptedAnswer.text: The foundational blocks of modern AI are centuries old. The mathematical concepts of linear regression and gradient descent were established in the 1800s, while the perceptron—a core building block of neural networks—was invented in the 1950s.

mainEntity.name: How was machine learning used in 2016?

acceptedAnswer.text: In 2016, machine learning was largely used for highly niche applications, such as Bioinformatics and FinTech. The applications were built exclusively for domain experts, and the training data had to be meticulously clean and structured, making implementation complex.

mainEntity.name: What caused the mass adoption of AI?

acceptedAnswer.text: The mass adoption of AI was driven primarily by UX convenience. Large Language Models (LLMs) combined with intuitive, user-friendly chat interfaces brought frictionless AI to the masses, shifting the technology from the realm of futurists to serious, everyday business.
